title: Grade Input — User Interaction
description: Extend your program to read a student's name using getline and three numeric grades using cin. Calculate the average of the three grades and display the result. This introduces variables, expressions, and user input.
test_code:
assert '#include <iostream>' in cpp, 'Must include <iostream> header'
assert '#include <string>' in cpp, 'Must include <string> header for string type'
assert 'cin' in cpp, 'Must use cin to read input'
assert 'getline' in cpp, 'Must use getline to read the student name'
assert '/' in cpp.split('main')[1], 'Must calculate an average using division (/)'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Must use cout to display the result'
assert 'string' in cpp, 'Must declare a string variable for the name'
acceptance_criteria: Program reads the student name with getline
Displays the student name and average grade
hints: Use: getline(cin, name); to read a full name with spaces
Declare grades as double: double grade1, grade2, grade3;
Average formula: double average = (grade1 + grade2 + grade3) / 3.0;
If mixing cin >> and getline, use cin.ignore() after cin >> to clear the newline
